methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 8 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $125M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: NODA AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Series A), post-money $125M

data.narrative:
Firehawk Aerospace is a Series A-stage defense and security technology company that has raised $25M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparables universe of 10 peers—2 public companies (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms across the same sector and stage cohort, including Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Code Metal, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $25M total raised as a proxy baseline and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression, implying a $50M valuation. Peer-median analysis examines the 8 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables, which span a range of $125M to $3.0B with a median of $1.0B, directly implying a $1.0B valuation. The wide peer range reflects heterogeneity in defense-tech maturity and defensibility at this stage.

Triangulating these two methods yields a low estimate of $42M, a median of $525M, and a high of $1.1B. The substantial spread reflects tension between stage-appropriate valuation anchors ($50M) and peer-median signals ($1.0B), indicating material uncertainty around Firehawk's positioning within the peer set. Key uncertainties include the company's relative technical differentiation, go-to-market traction, and whether peer valuations reflect sustainable fundamentals or stage-driven inflation in the frontier defense sector.
